member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMObject
member.summary: Represents the base CIM object class.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMObject.ArrayFromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs an array of CIMObjects from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the array of CIMObjects.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMObject.JsonFromArray(ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMObject[],ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIM objects and their current state.
member.param: The CIM objects to be written to JSON.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMObject.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMObject and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCMYKColor
member.summary: Represents a color in the CMYK color model.
member.remarks: A color in the CMYK (cyan, magenta, yellow, black) color model. CMYK is a subtractive color model commonly used in color printing.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCMYKColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMCMYKColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMCMYKColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCMYKColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMCMYKColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColor
member.summary: Supports colors in the CIM model by providing low level access to properties common amongst all color types.
member.remarks: This is the base color type which provides access to the color values and color space. Alpha is always the last value in the value array.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColor.ColorSpace
member.summary: Gets or sets the color space of the color. This color space is defined by an ICC color profile (except for spot colors which use a dedicated color space definition).
member.remarks: For color types for which color profiles are available (RGB, HSL, HSV and CMYK), the color space should be defined and its URL should match the color profile defined on the container (map, layout, report or style) for the given color type: RGB, HSL or HSV color => RGB color profile. CMYK color => CMYK color profile.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColor.Values
member.summary: Gets or sets the values for the color and alpha channels as defined by the color model. Alpha is the last value in the array for all colors.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColorRamp
member.summary: Supports color ramp schemes in the CIM model by providing low level access to properties common amongst all color ramp types.
member.remarks: This is the base color ramp type which provides access to the color space.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColorRamp.ColorSpace
member.summary: Gets or sets the color space of the color. This color space is defined by an ICC color profile.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMColorSpace
member.summary: Supports colors spaces by providing a common base type for all color spaces.
member.remarks: This is the base color space class.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType
member.summary: Specifies color spaces used for colors.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.RGB
member.summary: The RGB (red, green, blue) color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.CMYK
member.summary: The CMYK (cyan, magenta, yellow, black) color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.Gray
member.summary: The Grayscale color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.HSV
member.summary: The HSV (hue, saturation, value (brightness)) color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.HLS
member.summary: The HSL (hue, saturation, lightness) color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.XYZ
member.summary: The XYZ color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.LAB
member.summary: The LAB color space.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ColorSpaceType.Spot
member.summary: Spot color space.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMContinuousColorRamp
member.summary: Supports continuous color ramp schemes by providing low level access to properties common amongst all continuous color ramp types.
member.remarks: This is the base continuous color ramp type which primarily provides access to from and to colors.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMContinuousColorRamp.FromColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the beginning color for the color ramp (also known as a color scheme).

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMContinuousColorRamp.ToColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the ending color for the color ramp (also known as a color scheme). This is the color that the scheme transitions to.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMContinuousColorRamp.PrimitiveName
member.summary: Gets or sets the primitive name.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMFixedColorRamp
member.summary: Represents a color scheme composed of discrete colors.
member.remarks: A color scheme composed of an ordered list of discrete colors.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMFixedColorRamp.Colors
member.summary: Gets or sets the ordered list of colors in the color scheme.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMFixedColorRamp.Arrangement
member.summary: Gets or sets the arrangement type.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMFixedColorRamp.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMFixedColorRamp with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMFixedColorRamp state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMFixedColorRamp.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMFixedColorRamp and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.FixedColorRampArrangementType
member.summary: Specifies the arrangement type for fixed color ramp.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.FixedColorRampArrangementType.Default
member.summary: A fixed list of colors, typically used for unique value renderer.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.FixedColorRampArrangementType.Bivariate
member.summary: A fixed list of colors for bivariate rendering. The count of colors should be equal to the number of breaks for each axis multiplied.
member.remarks: For example, a 3x3 bivariate fixed list should have 9 colors in the order of A1, A2, A3, B1, B2, B3, C1, C2, C3 where values 1,2, and 3 correspond to classes in variable 1 and A, B, and C correspond to classes in variable 2.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGrayColor
member.summary: Represents a grayscale color defined by lightness.
member.remarks: A color in a grayscale model, where the value of lightness is defined.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGrayColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGrayColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGrayColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGrayColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGrayColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSLColor
member.summary: Represents a color defined by hue, saturation, and lightness.
member.remarks: Transforms RGB values by defining the hue, saturation, and lightness of the color. The three values do not represent individual color channels but the HSL coordinates used to define the color in the RGB color model.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSLColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMHSLColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMHSLColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSLColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMHSLColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSVColor
member.summary: Represents a color defined by hue, saturation, and brightness (value).
member.remarks: Transforms RGB values by defining the hue, saturation, and brightness (value) of the color. The three values do not represent individual color channels but the HSV coordinates used to define the color in the RGB color model.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSVColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMHSVColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMHSVColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHSVColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMHSVColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMICCColorSpace
member.summary: Represents a color space defined by an International Color Consortium (ICC) color profile.
member.remarks: A color space defined by an International Color Consortium (ICC) color profile.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMICCColorSpace.URL
member.summary: Gets or sets the URL of short name of the color space definition.
member.remarks: As a shortcut, the following strings can be set instead of full URLs: "Default RGB", "Default CMYK", "CIELAB", "CIEXYZ", and "Default Gray".

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMICCColorSpace.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMICCColorSpace with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMICCColorSpace state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMICCColorSpace.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMICCColorSpace and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLABColor
member.summary: Represents a color defined in the LAB color space.
member.remarks: Defines colors in the CIELAB color space, which is a color-opponent space with dimension L for lightness and a and b for the color-opponent dimensions.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLABColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMLABColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMLABColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLABColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMLABColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp
member.summary: Represents a linear continuous color ramp scheme.
member.remarks: A color ramp color scheme that has a linear transition between two colors. This ramp is created using the CIELAB algorithm which blends two colors without traversing the intervening hue space.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMLinearContinuousColorRamp and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMMultipartColorRamp
member.summary: Represents a multipart color ramp scheme.
member.remarks: A color ramp scheme defined by combining two or more continuous, discrete, or random ramps.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMMultipartColorRamp.ColorRamps
member.summary: Gets or sets the ordered list of color ramps (also known as schemes) that are combined to build the multipart ramp. Typically these are continuous color ramps.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMMultipartColorRamp.Weights
member.summary: Gets or sets the proportional weight for each color ramp (also known as a color scheme) that make up the multipart ramp. This allows for ramp to ramp transition at specific weight points.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMMultipartColorRamp.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMMultipartColorRamp with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMMultipartColorRamp state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMMultipartColorRamp.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMMultipartColorRamp and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp
member.summary: Represents a polar continuous color ramp scheme.
member.remarks: A color ramp scheme that has a polar transition between two colors. This scheme uses the HSV algorithm which uses a path around the color wheel to transition the hue, saturation, and value of the beginning color to the hue, saturation, and value of the ending color.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp.InterpolationSpace
member.summary: Gets or sets the color space in which the polar interpolation occurs. HSV, HLS, and LAB are the only supported color space types.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp.PolarDirection
member.summary: Gets or sets the direction of the polar progression for the path from the beginning hue to the ending hue.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMPolarContinuousColorRamp and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.PolarDirection
member.summary: Specifies the direction of the polar progression for the path from the beginning hue to the ending hue.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.PolarDirection.Auto
member.summary: The shortest path, clockwise or counter clockwise.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.PolarDirection.Clockwise
member.summary: The spectral order from red through orange, yellow, green, and blue to violet.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.PolarDirection.Counterclockwise
member.summary: The reverse spectral order from violet, through blue, green, yellow, and orange to red.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRGBColor
member.summary: Represents a color in the RGB color model.
member.remarks: A color in the RGB (red, green, blue) color model. RGB is an additive, or light-emissive model based on red, green, and blue values.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRGBColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMRGBColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMRGBColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRGBColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMRGBColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp
member.summary: Represents a random HSV color ramp scheme.
member.remarks: A color ramp scheme where the colors are randomly selected within the set range of hue, saturation, and value values.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MinH
member.summary: Gets or sets the hue from which the color ramp (also known as a color scheme) will start. Values can range from 0-360.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MaxH
member.summary: Gets or sets the hue at which the color ramp (also known as a color scheme) will end. Values can range from 0-360.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MinS
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum saturation possible for the colors within the color ramp (also known as a color scheme). Values can range between 0-100.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MaxS
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum saturation possible for the colors within the color ramp (also known as a color scheme). Values can range between 0-100.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MinV
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum value possible for the colors within the ramp (also known as a scheme). Values can range between 0-100.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MaxV
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum value possible for the colors within the color ramp (also known as a color scheme). Values can range between 0-100.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MinAlpha
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum alpha or transparency for all of the colors. Can be a value between 0-100, where 0 is fully transparent.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.MaxAlpha
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum alpha or transparency for all of the colors. Can be a value between 0-100, where 0 is fully transparent.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.Seed
member.summary: Gets or sets the starting point for the random generation of the colors.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMRandomHSVColorRamp with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMRandomHSVColorRamp state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMRandomHSVColorRamp.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMRandomHSVColorRamp and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColor
member.summary: Represents a spot color.
member.remarks: A color defined by a specific ink standard and typically used in offset printing where the color corresponds to a pure or premixed ink. When a spot color is displayed on the screen the alternative color is used.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColor.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the spot color name.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColor.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMSpotColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMSpotColor state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColor.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMSpotColor and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace
member.summary: Represents a color space for spot colors.
member.remarks: A color space defined for spot colors including alternate and book colors.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.Description
member.summary: Gets or sets a description of the ink. This can provide more information than the name.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the defined ink name for the spot color. Usually this is a well-known color standard name.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.AlternativeColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the standard (non-spot) color that is used to display the color on-screen.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.BookColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the if present, the color used during output.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.BookID
member.summary: Gets or sets the ID of the book the color name belongs to.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.IsReferencedColor
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether or not the color is a referenced color. When this property is true, the alternative color will be looked up from the color book based on the BookID and Name.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMSpotColorSpace with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMSpotColorSpace state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSpotColorSpace.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMSpotColorSpace and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMXYZColor
member.summary: Represents a color in the XYZ color model.
member.remarks: A color in the XYZ color space which is based on direct measurements of the human eye.

member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMXYZColor with a specified state from a JSON encoding.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMXYZColor and its current state.

member.summary: Gets or sets the transparency of the graphic as a percentage. Typically set by the layer or element during draw, but not persisted. Change the transparency of layers in the symbol for persistent changes.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMSymbolReference
member.summary: Represents a symbol reference.
member.remarks: Symbol references currently store the symbol in-line in the symbol property. Overrides here are used primarily by renderers to pass overrides through the drawing pipeline.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum scale range the symbol reference should be displayed at.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties
member.summary: Represents animated symbol properties, a collection of symbol properties that apply when the symbol layer has animation data.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.PlayAnimation
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the animated symbol should play its animation.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.ReverseAnimation
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the animated symbol should playback in reverse.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.RandomizeStartTime
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to generate a randomized start offset in seconds to apply to the symbol animation of each feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.RandomizeStartSeed
member.summary: Gets or sets the starting value for generating a random number. This random number is used by the randomizeStartTime property to determine time offset for each feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.StartTimeOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the time offset in seconds to use as the starting point of the symbol animation. This is used if randomizeStartTime=false.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.Duration
member.summary: Gets or sets how many seconds it takes to play through the symbol's animation once. This determines a symbol's playback speed.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.EndingDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets the ending duration of a symbol for time-aware data interpolation. The symbol's playback speed is interpolated linearly from duration to endingDuration over the display period of a time-aware feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.UseEndingDuration
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to use the ending duration of a symbol for time-aware data interpolation.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.RepeatType
member.summary: Gets or sets how to repeat the animation of a symbol when an animation cycle ends.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.RepeatDelay
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of seconds to delay before repeating an animation cycle.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.PrimitiveName
member.summary: Gets or sets the primitive name.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMAnimatedSymbolProperties.Easing
member.summary: Gets or sets the animation's easing. Only applicable on non file-based animations.

member.summary: Represents the ways an animated symbol can be repeated.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.AnimatedSymbolRepeatType.None
member.summary: When done playing an animation cycle, stop at the last frame.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.AnimatedSymbolRepeatType.Loop
member.summary: When done playing an animation cycle, repeat it from the beginning.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.AnimatedSymbolRepeatType.Oscillate
member.summary: When done playing an animation cycle, repeat it in reverse.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCallout
member.summary: Represents a callout.
member.remarks: Callouts are used to define additional ornamentation for a symbol. The primary use for callouts is to add leader line capabilities to text. Callouts are also used for defining items such as highway shields, balloon callouts, and more complex text box and leader line combinations. All callouts have some capability to draw leader lines; therefore they share a base property of a leader tolerance.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCallout.LeaderTolerance
member.summary: Gets or sets the leader tolerance which is the closest distance (in points) to the text the anchor point can be for the callout to draw.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCallout.LeaderOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the leader offset which is an offset value defining the distance (in points) between the anchor point and the beginning of the drawn leader.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker
member.summary: Represents a character marker.
member.remarks: The shape of a marker is defined by a glyph in a font. The marker is drawn using the specified size and color. Each marker has a defined frame around the glyph that is considered when the size is applied. As a result, character markers of the same size may appear different sizes if the glyphs frames are different sizes.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.CharacterIndex
member.summary: Gets or sets the Unicode decimal value for the individual glyph of the font that defines the shape of the marker.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.Depth3D
member.summary: Gets or sets the depth of the marker when drawn in 3D.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.FontFamilyName
member.summary: Gets or sets the font family name of the font. e.g. Comic Sans.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.FontStyleName
member.summary: Gets or sets the style name for the font family. e.g. Regular, Bold, or Italic.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.FontType
member.summary: Gets or sets the font type.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.FontVariationSettings
member.summary: Gets or sets an array of CIM font variation objects, describing a particular instance of a variable font through the font axis tags and their values. The number of CIM font variations will correspond to the number of variation axes specified by the font. This is only used for variable fonts.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.ScaleX
member.summary: Gets or sets the width of the symbol without changing the height (or depth in 3D), as a ratio.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.Symbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the polygon symbol that is used to renderer the marker.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.VerticalOrientation3D
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the marker stands a marker upright as though locked in place. The marker can be viewed from all angles.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.ScaleSymbolsProportionally
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the strokes and/or fills of a marker are scaled proportionally when the symbol size is changed.
member.remarks: When enabled, the strokes for the outline or fill of the polygon symbol used to draw the marker will be scaled proportionally with changes to the symbol size. If this property is not enabled, the stroke will draw with the specified width regardless of the marker size.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.RespectFrame
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the frame of the character marker should be honored when transforming the marker.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMCharacterMarker with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMCharacterMarker state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCharacterMarker.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMCharacterMarker and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout
member.summary: Represents a composite callout. Composite callouts are a filled background along with an optional shadow, which is placed behind text. They may also have a leader line (consisting of one or both of a simple line, and filled dart) connecting the callout to an anchor point. Composite callouts may have additional surrounding text elements. The text representing these parts is specified in the text string using tags. Their relative position properties are specified in the CIMCompositeCallout through their corresponding CIMCompositeTextPartPosition elements specified below, but these properties can also be overridden with tag attributes.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.CornerRadius
member.summary: Gets or sets the callout corner radius in points. 0.0 corresponds to a rectangle corner.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.BackgroundSymbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the symbol used to draw the background.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Margin
member.summary: Gets or sets the text margin defining the space around the text that is accounted for in balloon creation.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.DartWidth
member.summary: Gets or sets the dart width.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.DartSymbol
member.summary: Gets or sets a symbol used to draw the dart. This is only used if LeaderLinePercentage is less than 100%.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.SnapLeaderToCornersOnly
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the leader line should snap only to corners.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.LeaderLinePercentage
member.summary: Gets or sets the percentage of the leader line which is drawn as a line as opposed to a solid dart.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.LeaderLineSymbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the leader line symbol. This is only used if LeaderLinePercentage is greater than 0%.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.ShadowSymbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the shadow symbol for the callout.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.ShadowXOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the shadow offset from the callout symbol in the horizontal direction. If X and Y are zero, no shadow is drawn.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.ShadowYOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the shadow offset from the callout symbol in the vertical direction. If X and Y are zero, no shadow is drawn.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Middle
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the middle part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information. Split offset and box will have no effect on this text part, since it is required that the middle part is in the center of the callout.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.TopLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the top left part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Top
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the top part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.TopRight
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the top right part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Right
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the right part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Left
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the left part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.BottomLeft
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the bottom left part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Bottom
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the bottom part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.BottomRight
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the bottom right part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.Floating
member.summary: Gets or sets the text part position properties for the floating part of the callout text. This contains the relative positioning information, as well as split offset indicating the extent to which the callout part will mask the callout outline, and whether or not the part should be boxed in by the overall callout, or not. The floating part is anchored below the bottom text part. If there is no bottom text part it is anchored below the middle text part plus margins.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMCompositeCallout with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMCompositeCallout state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeCallout.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMCompositeCallout and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition
member.summary: Represents the text part position properties on a callout part.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.HorizontalAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the horizontal alignment of the text part.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.VerticalAlignment
member.summary: Gets or sets the vertical alignment of the text part.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.XOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the X offset of the text part.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.YOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the Y offset of the text part.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.SplitOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets the split offset of the text part. This determines how much of a gap there is between the callout border and any part of the text part intersecting the border If this is set to wider than the callout width, none of the associated callout line will draw.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.IsPartWithinCalloutBox
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the text part will be contained within the main callout. If this is set to true, the main callout will expand to encompass this text part, and a line will be draw separating the part from the rest of the callout. This line will have the same symbol as the callout outline and will only draw if the text part is above or below the top and bottom margins of the middle text.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMCompositeTextPartPosition with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMCompositeTextPartPosition state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMCompositeTextPartPosition.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMCompositeTextPartPosition and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ExtremityPlacement
member.summary: Extremity placement options which specify at which ends of the line a marker will be placed.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ExtremityPlacement.Both
member.summary: Both - marker is placed at the beginning and end of the line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ExtremityPlacement.JustBegin
member.summary: JustBegin - marker is placed at the beginning of the line, determined by the direction that the line was digitized.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ExtremityPlacement.JustEnd
member.summary: JustEnd - marker is placed at the end of the line, determined by the direction that the line was digitized.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.ExtremityPlacement.None
member.summary: None - no marker is placed at either end of the marker.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffect
member.summary: Represents a geometric effect, this is base class for all geometric effects.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffect.PrimitiveName
member.summary: Gets or sets the primitive name.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectAddControlPoints
member.summary: Represents the add control points geometric effect.
member.remarks: Dynamically adds geometry control points to a feature to dictate the placement of markers or other effect properties that leverage control points. Control points are placed at angles or deflection based on the AngleTolerance value.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectAddControlPoints.AngleTolerance
member.summary: Gets or sets the value below which a control point will be placed.
member.remarks: The maximum amount of deflection from one segment to another at a vertex. Angle values between 180 and 360 are interpreted the same as values between 0 and 180. Angle values of 180 and 360 are the same as 0.

member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectAddControlPoints with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectAddControlPoints state.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectAddControlPoints and its current state.

member.summary: Gets or sets the start angle of the circular sector. The angle is calculated in a counterclockwise manner with 0 degrees equal to due east.

member.summary: Gets or sets a width value.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule
member.summary: Specifies the rules to transform the input ground control points given as a line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.FullGeometry
member.summary: Default: returns the input line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PerpendicularFromFirstSegment
member.summary: Third vertex to first segment midpoint.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ReversedFirstSegment
member.summary: Line between vertex 2 and vertex 1. Line begins at vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PerpendicularToSecondSegment
member.summary: Second segment midpoint to first vertex.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.SecondSegmentWithTicks
member.summary: Line between vertex 2 and vertex 3 with ticks on ends.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.DoublePerpendicular
member.summary: Two lines perpendicular to the first segment. Length of the lines is determined by vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.OppositeToFirstSegment
member.summary: Lines perpendicular to vertex 3 and the midpoint of the first segment with the length determined by first segment.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.TriplePerpendicular
member.summary: Three lines perpendicular to the first segment. Length of the lines is determined by vertex 3. Top and bottom lines stop at 80% of first segment.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.HalfCircleFirstSegment
member.summary: Half circle centered on the midpoint of first segment. Orientation is opposite vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.HalfCircleSecondSegment
member.summary: Half circle ending on vertex 2 opposite vertex 1. Diameter is defined by vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.HalfCircleExtended
member.summary: U shaped line with curve between vertex 2 and vertex 3. Control points on vertex 1 and vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.OpenCircle
member.summary: 330 degrees circle centered on vertex 1 starting at vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.CoverageEdgesWithTicks
member.summary: Lines between vertex 1 to vertex 3 and vertex 2 to vertex 4. Angled ticks at the end of the lines and vertex 1 and vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.GapExtentWithDoubleTicks
member.summary: Two lines with angled ticks on end. Lines created between first and third segments.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.GapExtentMidline
member.summary: Line centered between first and third segment.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.Chevron
member.summary: Chevron with apex at vertex 1 and ends at vertex 2 and vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PerpendicularWithArc
member.summary: Line between vertex 1 and vertex 2 with arc off of vertex 2. Cross line is perpendicular to vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ClosedHalfCircle
member.summary: Semi-circle with vertex 1 and vertex 2 at the corners.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.TripleParallelExtended
member.summary: Three parallel lines of varying length connected to a base line. The base defined by first segment. Vertex 3 is the end of the longest line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ParallelWithTicks
member.summary: Line with angled ticks on ends between vertex 1 and vertex 2 with a mirrored parallel line placed at the width defined by vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.Parallel
member.summary: Line between vertex 1 and vertex 2 with a parallel line placed at the width defined by vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PerpendicularToFirstSegment
member.summary: Line perpendicular to the midpoint of first segment. Length X% longer than vertex 2-vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ParallelOffset
member.summary: Parallels lines offset from first segment. Offset determined by vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.OffsetOpposite
member.summary: Single line offset from first segment. Offset to opposite side as vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.OffsetSame
member.summary: Single line offset from first segment. Offset to same side as vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.CircleWithArc
member.summary: Circle centered on vertex 1 with attached arc. Vertex 3 determines the side of the 90 degree arc. If 3 points, vertex 2 determines the length of the arc. If 4 points, vertex 2 determines the radius of the circle and vertex 4 determines the length of the arc.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.DoubleJog
member.summary: Two lines with jogs with gap in between. If there is 3 points the gap is centered on vertex. If 4 points the gap is defined by second segment.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PerpendicularOffset
member.summary: Line perpendicular from midpoint of second segment. End point is offset from second segment. Vertex 1 sets length of line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.LineExcludingLastSegment
member.summary: Line between vertex 1 and vertex N-1.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.MultivertexArrow
member.summary: Multipoint arrow. Vertex 1 sets the tip and vertex N the width and back of the arrowhead.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.CrossedArrow
member.summary: Multipoint arrow. Vertex 1 sets the tip and vertex N the width and back of the arrowhead. Crossed at midpoint between vertex 1 and vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ChevronArrow
member.summary: Chevron arrow centered on vertex 1.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ChevronArrowOffset
member.summary: Chevron arrow centered on vertex 1 offset towards vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.PartialFirstSegment
member.summary: Line along first segment, starting at vertex 2 extending in the direction and length of vertex 3.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.Arch
member.summary: 3 point arched line between vertex 1 and vertex 2. Vertex 3 determines the depth of the arch.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.CurvedParallelTicks
member.summary: Curved line with ticks. Curve begins on vertex 2 and ends on vertex 3. Ticks on opposite side of vertex 1.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.Arc90Degrees
member.summary: 90 degree arc with ends at vertex 1 and vertex 2. Vertex 3 determines the side of the curve.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.TipWithPerpendicularAndTicks
member.summary: Dynamic line perpendicular to segment 1, center on vertex 1 and offset towards vertex 2 with ticks.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ConcentricCircles
member.summary: Creates 2 or 3 concentric circles centered on vertex 1. Circle radius is based on vertex location.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.DoubleJogArrow
member.summary: Creates two dynamic lines with jogs centered on vertex 1.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.LinkedChevrons
member.summary: Creates a dynamic line between vertex 1 and 2 connecting two chevrons. Vertex 3 defines the width of the chevrons.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.SegmentThenHalfCircle
member.summary: Creates a dynamic line with a straight segment followed by a half circle. The diameter of the circle is determined by the length of the second segment. Vertex 4 determines the side of the half circle.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.LineWithStraightTicks
member.summary: Creates a dynamic line with vertex 1 in the middle. Vertices 2 and 3 define the length of the ticks and the line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.DoubleCurve
member.summary: Creates a dynamic line with alternating curves at vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ParallelWithTicksByWidth
member.summary: Creates a dynamic line with angled ticks on the ends between vertex 2 and vertex 3. A mirrored parallel line is placed at the width defined by vertex 2.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.EnclosingRoundedRectangle
member.summary: Creates an enclosing rectangle around the line with rounded corners.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectControlMeasureLineRule.ParallelOffsetByValue
member.summary: Creates dynamic parallel lines offset from the geometry. The offset is determined by a specified value or a defined attribute.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut
member.summary: Represents the cut geometric effect which creates a dynamic line that is shorter on one or both ends than the line feature or polygon outline.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.BeginCut
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance from the beginning of a line that the display of the stroke starts. The beginning of the line is determined by the direction in which the line was digitized.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.EndCut
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance from the end of a line that the display of the stroke ends. The end of the line is determined by the direction in which the line was digitized.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.MiddleCut
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance around the middle of a line that the display of the stroke is interrupted.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.Invert
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the effect should be applied in the opposite manner. This displays the stroke symbol only at the ends of the line and leaves the rest of the line unsymbolized.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectCut with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectCut state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectCut.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectCut and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes
member.summary: Represents the dashes geometric effect which creates a dynamic multipart line geometry from a line feature or the outline of a polygon based on a template.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.CustomEndingOffset
member.summary: Gets or sets where the pattern should end relative to the ending point of the geometry. Negative numbers indicate a shift to the left and positive numbers a shift to the right. This property is only applied if the LineDashEnding is set to Custom.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.DashTemplate
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance for each dash and gap. There can be multiple dash and gap values to form a complex pattern.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.LineDashEnding
member.summary: Gets or sets the setting which determines how the strokes with dash patterns and other patterns (pictures, placement effects) are handled at the end points of the line geometry's segments.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.OffsetAlongLine
member.summary: Gets or sets the position where the pattern should begin relative to the starting point of the geometry. It shifts the entire pattern along the line the specified distance. Negative values indicate a shift to the left and positive numbers a shift to the right. This property is only applied if LineDashEnding is set to NoConstraint or Custom.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.ControlPointEnding
member.summary: Gets or sets the line dash ending position.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectDashes with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectDashes state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDashes.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectDashes and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut
member.summary: Represents the donut geometric effect which creates a dynamic polygon ring of a specified width in relation to the outline of polygon features.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the method which specifies the way the strokes are displayed at convex corners of the polygon.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut.Option
member.summary: Gets or sets the option for the way the symbol handles complex geometries.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut.Width
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance from the edge of the polygon that the fill symbol is to be displayed.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectDonut with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectDonut state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectDonut.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectDonut and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod
member.summary: Geometric effect donut methods.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod.Mitered
member.summary: Mitered - matches the exact shape around a convex corner of the polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod.Bevelled
member.summary: Bevelled - follows the shortest straight path across a convex corner of the polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod.Rounded
member.summary: Rounded - follows a path of equal distance around a convex corner of the polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod.Square
member.summary: Square - follows a straight path across the corner of a line or polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectDonutMethod.TrueBuffer
member.summary: TrueBuffer - uses the buffer algorithm to follow a path around convex corners.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon
member.summary: Represents the enclosing polygon geometric effect which creates a dynamic polygon from the spatial extent of a line or polygon feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the method which specifies the way in which the polygon geometry is generated around the feature geometry.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectEnclosingPolygon and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectEnclosingPolygonMethod
member.summary: Geometric effect enclosing polygon methods.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectEnclosingPolygonMethod.ClosePath
member.summary: ClosePath - for polygon input, it generates a polygon that matches the geometry of a polygon feature. For line input, it generates a polygon that connects both ends of the line to each other.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectEnclosingPolygonMethod.ConvexHull
member.summary: ConvexHull - for polygon input, it generates a polygon with a minimum number of sides to surround the feature. For line input, it generates a polygon that approximates the shape of the line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectEnclosingPolygonMethod.RectangularBox
member.summary: RectangularBox - generates a polygon equal to the spatial envelope of the feature.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectExtension
member.summary: Represents the extension geometric effect which creates a dynamic line that is extended from either the beginning or the end of the line feature at a specified deflection angle and length.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectExtension.Deflection
member.summary: Gets or sets the deflection angle used for the extension. A value of 0 indicates no deflection.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectExtension.Origin
member.summary: Gets or sets the origin of the extension to add to the line. The beginning and end of the line is defined by the direction the line was digitized.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectExtension.Length
member.summary: Gets or sets the length of the extension that is dynamically created.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectExtension and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectExtensionOrigin
member.summary: Geometric effect extension origins. Specifies the origin of the extension to add to the line. The beginning and end of the line is defined by the direction the line was digitized.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectExtensionOrigin.BeginningOfLine
member.summary: BeginningOfLine - extension is added to the end of the line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectExtensionOrigin.EndOfLine
member.summary: EndOfLine - extension is added to the end of the line.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectJog
member.summary: Represents the jog geometric effect which creates a dynamic line with a jog of a specified angle, position, and width in the line.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectJog.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the angle of the jog in the line which is measured in degrees.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectJog.Length
member.summary: Gets or sets the length of the segment that forms the jog in the line.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectJog.Position
member.summary: Gets or sets the location of the center of the jog, as a percentage measured from the start of the input geometry.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectJog and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetMethod
member.summary: Geometric effect offset method which specifies the way the strokes or fills are displayed at corners.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetMethod.Mitered
member.summary: Mitered - matches the exact shape around a corner of the line or polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetMethod.Bevelled
member.summary: Bevelled - follows the shortest straight path across a corner of the line or polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetMethod.Rounded
member.summary: Rounded - follows a path of equal distance around a corner of the line or polygon.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetMethod.Square
member.summary: Square - follows a straight path across the corner of a line or polygon.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetOption
member.summary: Geometric effect offset options which specify the way the symbol handles complex geometries.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetOption.Fast
member.summary: Fast - ignores complex geometries and applies a best fit to the symbol.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetOption.Accurate
member.summary: Accurate - accommodates complex geometries and applied a true fit to the symbol.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectOffsetTangent
member.summary: Represents the offset tangent geometric effect which creates a dynamic line along a line feature offset in the direction defined by either the beginning or the end of the line.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectOffsetTangent.Method
member.summary: Gets or sets the origin of the tangent offset for the line. The beginning and end of the lines are defined by how the line was digitized.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectOffsetTangent.Offset
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance the geometry is moved tangent.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetTangentMethod
member.summary: Geometric effect offset tangent methods which specify the origin of the tangent offset for the line. The beginning and end of the lines are defined by how the line was digitized.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetTangentMethod.BeginningOfLine
member.summary: BeginningOfLine - the tangent offset is applied from the beginning of the line.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GeometricEffectOffsetTangentMethod.EndOfLine
member.summary: EndOfLine - the tangent offset is applied from the end of the line.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectRadial
member.summary: Represents the radial geometric effect which creates a dynamic line of a specified length and angle originating from a point feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectRadial.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the orientation of the line from the marker. The angle is calculated in a counterclockwise manner with 0 degrees equal to due east.

member.summary: Gets or sets the distance of the line from end to end.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectRegularPolygon
member.summary: Represents the regular polygon geometric effect which creates a dynamic polygon around a point feature with a specified number of edges. All edges are equal in length and all angles are equal.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectRegularPolygon.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of rotation for the polygon.

member.summary: Gets or sets the number of sides for the polygon. Specifying a value less than 3 produces a circle.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectRegularPolygon and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectReverse
member.summary: Represents the reverse geometric effect which creates a dynamic polygon around a point feature with a specified number of edges. All edges are equal in length and all angles are equal.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the dynamic output of a previous geometric effect is to be flipped or not.

member.summary: Represents the rotate geometric effect which creates a dynamic line or polygon rotated a specified angle from the feature.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectRotate.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of rotation for the symbol.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectScale
member.summary: Represents the rotate geometric effect which creates a dynamic line or polygon scaled by a specified factor. Vertices are moved in relation to the center point of a feature envelope. Values greater than 1 move vertices away from the center point. Values between 0 and 1 move vertices toward the center point. Values less than 0 draw an inverse dynamic line or polygon where the vertices have crossed to the other side of the center point.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectScale.XScaleFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of change in size of a symbol in the x-axis. The value is expressed in terms of a ratio/percentage.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectScale.YScaleFactor
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount of change in size of a symbol in the y-axis. The value is expressed in terms of a ratio/percentage.

member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGeometricEffectScale with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGeometricEffectScale state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectScale.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectScale and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectSuppress
member.summary: Represents the suppress geometric effect which creates a dynamic line that hides sections of a stroke between pairs control points.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectSuppress.Suppress
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the portion of the stroke symbol between control points should be suppressed. Sections that are suppressed draw with no symbol.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectSuppress.Invert
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether to invert the suppression process. If this value is true, portions of the stroke symbol between control points are kept and all other portions are suppressed.

member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGeometricEffectSuppress and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGeometricEffectWave

member.summary: Gradient alignment types.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientAlignment.Buffered
member.summary: Buffered - Distributes the color ramp along the line's geometry from the outside in (similar to the effect of creating a buffer of the line, then using the "buffer" fill type).

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientAlignment.Left
member.summary: Left - Progresses the color ramp from the line's centerline to the outside edge on the left. The gradient will follow any curvature in the line's geometry.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientAlignment.Right
member.summary: Right - Progresses the color ramp from the line's centerline to the outside edge on the right. The gradient will follow any curvature in the line's geometry.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientAlignment.AlongLine
member.summary: Along line - Distributes the color ramp linearly along the line, following the curvature of the line.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill
member.summary: Represents a gradient fill which fills polygonal geometry with a specified color scheme.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.Angle
member.summary: Gets or sets the angle of the gradient when the GradientMethod is set to Linear or Rectangular.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.ColorRamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the color scheme that is applied to the fill.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.GradientMethod
member.summary: Gets or sets a value which specifies how the gradient is applied within the fill.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.GradientSize
member.summary: Gets or sets a value which determines how much of the feature is covered by the color scheme. This is either a percentage of the total area which the color scheme spans or the number of page units from the starting point at which the gradient displays.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.GradientSizeUnits
member.summary: Gets or sets a value which specifies whether GradientSize is applied with an absolute distance or a relative percentage.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.GradientType
member.summary: Gets or sets a value which specifies if the gradient is applied with discrete intervals or if it is continuous.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.Interval
member.summary: Gets or sets how many bands draw when the GradientType is set to Discrete.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.FromJson(System.String,ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonDeserializationSettings)
member.summary: Reconstructs the CIMGradientFill with a specified state from a JSON encoding.
member.param: The JSON string containing the CIMGradientFill state.

member.name: M:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientFill.ToJson(ArcGIS.Core.CIM.JsonSerializationSettings)
member.summary: Creates a JSON encoding of the CIMGradientFill and its current state.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientFillMethod
member.summary: Describes the gradient fill method which is how the gradient is applied.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientFillMethod.Linear
member.summary: Linear - Change from one color to the next is linear across the geometry.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientFillMethod.Rectangular
member.summary: Rectangular - Change from one color to the next is rectangular, from the extent of the geometry.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientFillMethod.Circular
member.summary: Circular - Change from one color to the next is circular, from the extent of the geometry

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientFillMethod.Buffered
member.summary: Buffered - Color changes based on an internal buffering of the geometry outline

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke
member.summary: Represents a gradient stroke which draws linear geometry with a specified color scheme.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.ColorRamp
member.summary: Gets or sets the color scheme that is applied to the stroke.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.GradientMethod
member.summary: Gets or sets how the gradient is applied along the stroke.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.GradientSize
member.summary: Gets or sets how much of the feature is covered by the color scheme. This is either a percentage of the total area which the color scheme spans or the number of page units from the starting point at which the gradient displays.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.GradientSizeUnits
member.summary: Gets or sets whether GradientSize is applied with an absolute distance or a relative percentage.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.GradientType
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the gradient is applied with discrete or continuous intervals.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMGradientStroke.Interval
member.summary: Gets or sets how many bands draw when the GradientType is set to Discrete.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientStrokeType
member.summary: Gradient stroke types.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientStrokeType.Discrete
member.summary: Discrete gradient types have distinct lines of separation between each color.

member.name: F:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.GradientStrokeType.Continuous
member.summary: Continuous gradients vary continuously along the color change with no distinct boundary between the colors.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill
member.summary: Represents a hatch fill which fills polygonal geometry with a uniform series of parallel line symbols.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill.LineSymbol
member.summary: Gets or sets the line symbol that is used to draw the hatch lines in the fill.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill.OffsetX
member.summary: Gets or sets how much to move the stroke to a new X-position.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill.Rotation
member.summary: Gets or sets the angle of rotation for all the strokes, in degrees.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill.Separation
member.summary: Gets or sets the distance between the line symbols in the hatch pattern.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.CIM.CIMHatchFill.OffsetY
member.summary: Gets or sets how much to move the stroke to a new Y-position.
